behaviors.description: Evaluate-stage command writes grounding to `grounding` artifact, not `plan`.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact."
Slot B: Boundary is command->artifact-store wiring (artifact key selection at write boundary).
Slot C: A Unit test of key-selection helper can pass while integration wiring still writes to `plan`.
Final label: Integration, because Slot B is an inter-module write boundary and Slot C shows Unit miss risk. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-governed handoff required. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected by "stores grounding ... artifact" (storage routing, not model judgment).
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate command with in-process artifact store + run context; assert new/updated artifact is `grounding` and `plan` is unchanged by this step. Fail if any write lands in `plan`.

behaviors.description: Implement-stage reads `grounding` when present and writes execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`."
Slot B: Boundary is artifact-reader + implement orchestrator + artifact-writer interaction.
Slot C: Unit tests of fallback/read helpers won’t catch end-to-end misrouting (read from wrong slot or write to wrong slot).
Final label: Integration, because Slot B is cross-module handoff and Slot C shows Unit is too low. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) behavior targets routing/orchestration not model-output correctness, (c) single-stage behavior, (d) no multi-stage LLM handoff assertion. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected by "reads grounding ... writes ... to `plan`" (I/O contract, not quality of generated content).
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact; execute implement flow with deterministic model stub; assert it reads grounding input path and emits/updates only `plan` for implementation output.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive stops overwriting `plan` when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content."
Slot B: Boundary is issue-content inspection -> deep-dive side-effect policy -> artifact write suppression.
Slot C: Unit test of the predicate (diagnosis exists) can pass while deep-dive command still writes `plan` due to orchestration bug.
Final label: Integration, because Slot B is policy+writer wiring and Slot C shows Unit miss. Integration-brake: (a) no OS/network/subprocess need, (b) no model-output quality dependency stated, (c) no multi-agentic sequence required, (d) not a multi-LLM handoff chain behavior. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected by "Deep-dive ... side effect" (single-stage side-effect constraint).
behaviors.test_description: Prepare issue content containing diagnosis; run deep-dive stage with artifact store attached; assert no `plan` write occurs from deep-dive path.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read prior `grounding` in same run context.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context."
Slot B: Boundary is cross-stage persistence (evaluate write) -> run-context lookup -> implement read.
Slot C: Unit tests of lineage IDs or lookup helpers miss real breakage in composed context/store wiring.
Final label: Integration, because Slot B is inter-module state handoff and Slot C shows adjacent-lower insufficiency. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required by text, (b) no dependence on AI output correctness, (c) no requirement for multiple real model decisions, (d) orchestration/state handoff exists but not LLM-decision correctness itself. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected by "artifact lineage is preserved" (data plumbing contract, not model judgment).
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate stage to create `grounding` under a run id, then execute implement in same run id; assert implement resolves and consumes that exact artifact lineage reference.

behaviors.description: In full chain (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), final plan reflects earlier grounding.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run."
Slot B: Boundary is multi-step agent pipeline with sequential stage decisions affecting downstream prompt/context.
Slot C: Integration with fixed stubs can pass while real staged agent decisions fail to carry/reflect grounding.
Final label: Workflow, because Slot B explicitly requires multi-step agentic sequencing and Slot C shows lower-level tests hide real pipeline failures. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected by "full multi-step agent run" + "reflects grounding produced earlier" (requires end-to-end sequential agent behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Run the real chain end-to-end with actual agent steps; assert final implement plan includes grounding-derived elements introduced upstream, verifying cross-step propagation through the full pipeline.

behaviors.description: If grounding is absent, implement safely falls back and still emits valid `plan` without corrupting other artifacts.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts."
Slot B: Boundary is missing-artifact handling + fallback path + selective artifact write integrity.
Slot C: Unit tests of fallback function miss corruption bugs in shared artifact store updates.
Final label: Integration, because Slot B spans fallback/orchestration/store modules and Slot C shows Unit gap. Integration-brake: (a) no OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no model-output quality dependency required, (c) no multi-agentic chain required, (d) not testing LLM decision handoff correctness. Rejection-gate: System rejected by behavior text focusing on artifact-state safety, not real infra effects.
behaviors.test_description: Start with run context lacking `grounding` but containing other artifacts; run implement; assert new/updated `plan` is valid and all non-target artifacts remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
